req.title: Allergy intolerance
req.statement: A clinician shall record an AllergyIntolerance linked to the patient; subsequent MedicationRequests shall warn on conflict.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for AllergyIntolerance recording and MedicationRequest conflict warnings.

Requirement:
    A clinician shall record an AllergyIntolerance linked to the patient;
    subsequent MedicationRequests shall warn on conflict.

Assumptions / design decisions
--------------------------------
* The system under test exposes a thin Python service layer (not raw HTTP)
  so that the tests remain fast and deterministic.  Replace the imports and
  fixtures with your actual client / SDK as needed.
* `AllergyIntoleranceService` and `MedicationRequestService` are the
  production classes being tested.
* Conflict detection returns a list of `ConflictWarning` objects; an empty
  list means no conflict.
* All identifiers are plain strings (e.g., SNOMED / RxNorm codes) so the
  tests carry no real terminology dependency.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import List, Optional
from datetime import date

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ---- Minimal domain models (replace with your real imports) ----------------
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@dataclass
class Patient:
    patient_id: str
    name: str


@dataclass
class AllergyIntolerance:
    allergy_id: str
    patient_id: str
    substance_code: str          # e.g. RxNorm / SNOMED code
    substance_display: str
    clinical_status: str         # "active" | "inactive" | "resolved"
    verification_status: str     # "confirmed" | "unconfirmed" | "refuted"
    recorded_by: str             # clinician id
    recorded_date: date = field(default_factory=date.today)
    note: Optional[str] = None


@dataclass
class MedicationRequest:
    request_id: str
    patient_id: str
    medication_code: str         # RxNorm code
    medication_display: str
    requested_by: str            # clinician id
    status: str = "active"       # "active" | "cancelled" | "on-hold"


@dataclass
class ConflictWarning:
    allergy_id: str
    medication_request_id: str
    substance_code: str
    medication_code: str
    severity: str                # "high" | "medium" | "low"
    message: str


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ---- Minimal service interfaces (replace with your real imports) -----------
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class AllergyIntoleranceService:
    """Manages CRUD for AllergyIntolerance resources."""

    def record(self, allergy: AllergyIntolerance) -> AllergyIntolerance:
        raise NotImplementedError

    def get_by_patient(self, patient_id: str) -> List[AllergyIntolerance]:
        raise NotImplementedError

    def get_by_id(self, allergy_id: str) -> Optional[AllergyIntolerance]:
        raise NotImplementedError

    def update(self, allergy: AllergyIntolerance) -> AllergyIntolerance:
        raise NotImplementedError

    def delete(self, allergy_id: str) -> bool:
        raise NotImplementedError


class MedicationRequestService:
    """Manages MedicationRequests and conflict checking."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        allergy_service: AllergyIntoleranceService,
    ):
        self._allergy_service = allergy_service

    def check_conflicts(
        self, medication_request: MedicationRequest
    ) -> List[ConflictWarning]:
        raise NotImplementedError

    def create(
        self, medication_request: MedicationRequest
    ) -> tuple[MedicationRequest, List[ConflictWarning]]:
        """
        Persist the request and return it alongside any conflict warnings.
        The caller decides whether to proceed; warnings do NOT block creation.
        """
        raise NotImplementedError


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ---- Fixtures --------------------------------------------------------------
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def patient() -> Patient:
    return Patient(patient_id="P001", name="Jane Doe")


@pytest.fixture
def clinician_id() -> str:
    return "CLINICIAN-42"


@pytest.fixture
def penicillin_allergy(patient: Patient, clinician_id: str) -> AllergyIntolerance:
    return AllergyIntolerance(
        allergy_id="AI-001",
        patient_id=patient.patient_id,
        substance_code="372687004",      # SNOMED: Penicillin
        substance_display="Penicillin",
        clinical_status="active",
        verification_status="confirmed",
        recorded_by=clinician_id,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def amoxicillin_request(patient: Patient, clinician_id: str) -> MedicationRequest:
    """Amoxicillin is a penicillin-class antibiotic → should trigger conflict."""
    return MedicationRequest(
        request_id="MR-001",
        patient_id=patient.patient_id,
        medication_code="723",           # RxNorm: Amoxicillin
        medication_display="Amoxicillin 500 mg",
        requested_by=clinician_id,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def metformin_request(patient: Patient, clinician_id: str) -> MedicationRequest:
    """Metformin — no known conflict with penicillin allergy."""
    return MedicationRequest(
        request_id="MR-002",
        patient_id=patient.patient_id,
        medication_code="6809",          # RxNorm: Metformin
        medication_display="Metformin 500 mg",
        requested_by=clinician_id,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def allergy_service() -> MagicMock:
    return MagicMock(spec=AllergyIntoleranceService)


@pytest.fixture
def medication_service(allergy_service: MagicMock) -> MagicMock:
    svc = MagicMock(spec=MedicationRequestService)
    svc._allergy_service = allergy_service
    return svc


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ---- Helper builders -------------------------------------------------------
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def make_conflict_warning(
    allergy: AllergyIntolerance,
    med_request: MedicationRequest,
    severity: str = "high",
) -> ConflictWarning:
    return ConflictWarning(
        allergy_id=allergy.allergy_id,
        medication_request_id=med_request.request_id,
        substance_code=allergy.substance_code,
        medication_code=med_request.medication_code,
        severity=severity,
        message=(
            f"Patient has recorded allergy to '{allergy.substance_display}'. "
            f"'{med_request.medication_display}' may cause a reaction."
        ),
    )


# ===========================================================================
# == TEST CLASSES ============================================================
# ===========================================================================


class TestRecordAllergyIntolerance:
    """Tests that a clinician can record an AllergyIntolerance for a patient."""

    def test_record_allergy_returns_persisted_object(
        self,
        allergy_service: MagicMock,
        penicillin_allergy: AllergyIntolerance,
    ):
        """Recorded allergy is returned by the service."""
        allergy_service.record.return_value = penicillin_allergy

        result = allergy_service.record(penicillin_allergy)

        allergy_service.record.assert_called_once_with(penicillin_allergy)
        assert result.allergy_id == penicillin_allergy.allergy_id

    def test_recorded_allergy_is_linked_to_patient(
        self,
        allergy_service: MagicMock,
        penicillin_allergy: AllergyIntolerance,
        patient: Patient,
    ):
        """The allergy must reference the correct patient."""
        allergy_service.record.return_value = penicillin_allergy

        result = allergy_service.record(penicillin_allergy)

        assert result.patient_id == patient.patient_id

    def test_recorded_allergy_has_substance_code(
        self,
        allergy_service: MagicMock,
        penicillin_allergy: AllergyIntolerance,
    ):
        """The allergy must carry a substance code."""
        allergy_service.record.return_value = penicillin_allergy

        result = allergy_service.record(penicillin_allergy)

        assert result.substance_code, "Substance code must not be empty"

    def test_recorded_allergy_stores_clinician(
        self,
        allergy_service: MagicMock,
        penicillin_allergy: AllergyIntolerance,
        clinician_id: str,
    ):
        """The recording clinician's id is persisted."""
        allergy_service.record.return_value = penicillin_allergy

        result = allergy_service.record(penicillin_allergy)

        assert result.recorded_by == clinician_id

    def test_recorded_allergy_has_active_clinical_status(
        self,
        allergy_service: MagicMock,
        penicillin_allergy: AllergyIntolerance,
    ):
        """A newly recorded allergy should default to 'active'."""
        allergy_service.record.return_value = penicillin_allergy

        result = allergy_service.record(penicillin_allergy)

        assert result.clinical_status == "active"

    def test_recorded_allergy_has_verification_status(
        self,
        allergy_service: MagicMock,
        penicillin_allergy: AllergyIntolerance,
    ):
        """Verification status must be present and valid."""
        valid_statuses = {"confirmed", "unconfirmed", "refuted"}
        allergy_service.record.return_value = penicillin_allergy

        result = allergy_service.record(penicillin_allergy)

        assert result.verification_status in valid_statuses

    def test_record_allergy_without_patient_id_raises(
        self,
        allergy_service: MagicMock,
        penicillin_allergy: AllergyIntolerance,
    ):
        """Recording an allergy without a patient link must raise ValueError."""
        allergy_service.record.side_effect = ValueError("patient_id is required")
        invalid_allergy = AllergyIntolerance(
            allergy_id="AI-BAD",
            patient_id="",               # empty — invalid
            substance_code="372687004",
            substance_display="Penicillin",
            clinical_status="active",
            verification_status="confirmed",
            recorded_by="CLINICIAN-42",
        )

        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="patient_id is required"):
            allergy_service.record(invalid_allergy)

    def test_record_allergy_without_substance_code_raises(
        self,
        allergy_service: MagicMock,
    ):
        """Recording an allergy without a substance code must raise ValueError."""
        allergy_service.record.side_effect = ValueError("substance_code is required")
        invalid_allergy = AllergyIntolerance(
            allergy_id="AI-BAD2",
            patient_id="P001",
            substance_code="",           # empty — invalid
            substance_display="Unknown",
            clinical_status="active",
            verification_status="unconfirmed",
            recorded_by="CLINICIAN-42",
        )

        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="substance_code is required"):
            allergy_service.record(invalid_allergy)

    def test_multiple_allergies_can_be_recorded_for_same_patient(
        self,
        allergy_service: MagicMock,
        patient: Patient,
        clinician_id: str,
        penicillin_allergy: AllergyIntolerance,
    ):
        """A patient may have more than one allergy on record."""
        latex_allergy = AllergyIntolerance(
            allergy_id="AI-002",
            patient_id=patient.patient_id,
            substance_code="1003754008",  # SNOMED: Latex
            substance_display="Latex",
            clinical_status="active",
            verification_status="confirmed",
            recorded_by=clinician_id,
        )
        allergy_service.get_by_patient.return_value = [
            penicillin_allergy,
            latex_allergy,
        ]

        results = allergy_service.get_by_patient(patient.patient_id)

        assert len(results) == 2
        patient_ids = {a.patient_id for a in results}
        assert patient_ids == {patient.patient_id}

    def test_allergy_can_be_retrieved_by_id(
        self,
        allergy_service: MagicMock,
        penicillin_allergy: AllergyIntolerance,
    ):
        """Recorded allergy is retrievable by its identifier."""
        allergy_service.get_by_id.return_value = penicillin_allergy

        result = allergy_service.get_by_id(penicillin_allergy.allergy_id)

        assert result is not None
        assert result.allergy_id == penicillin_allergy.allergy_id


class TestMedicationRequestConflictDetection:
    """
    Tests that a MedicationRequest triggers a warning when the patient has
    a recorded AllergyIntolerance that conflicts with the requested medication.
    """

    # --- check_conflicts ----------------------------------------------------

    def test_conflict_detected_for_known_allergenic_medication(
        self,
        medication_service: MagicMock,
        penicillin_allergy: AllergyIntolerance,
        amoxicillin_request: MedicationRequest,
    ):
        """Requesting amoxicillin for a penicillin-allergic patient raises a warning."""
        expected_warning = make_conflict_warning(penicillin_allergy, amoxicillin_request)
        medication_service.check_conflicts.return_value = [expected_warning]

        warnings = medication_service.check_conflicts(amoxicillin_request)

        assert len(warnings) == 1
        assert warnings[0].allergy_id == penicillin_allergy.allergy_id
        assert warnings[0].medication_request_id == amoxicillin_request.request_id

    def test_no_conflict_for_unrelated_medication(
        self,
        medication_service: MagicMock,
        metformin_request: MedicationRequest,
    ):
        """Requesting metformin for a penicillin-allergic patient → no warning."""
        medication_service.check_conflicts.return_value = []

        warnings = medication_service.check_conflicts(metformin_request)

        assert warnings == []

    def test_conflict_warning_contains_severity(
        self,
        medication_service: MagicMock,
        penicillin_allergy: AllergyIntolerance,
        amoxicillin_request: MedicationRequest,
    ):
        """Each conflict warning must carry a severity level."""
        warning = make_conflict_warning(penicillin_allergy, amoxicillin_request, "high")
        medication_service.check_conflicts.return_value = [warning]

        warnings = medication_service.check_conflicts(amoxicillin_request)

        assert warnings[0].severity in {"high", "medium", "low"}

    def test_conflict_warning_contains_human_readable_message(
        self,
        medication_service: MagicMock,
        penicillin_allergy: AllergyIntolerance,
        amoxicillin_request: MedicationRequest,
    ):
        """The warning must include a non-empty human-readable message."""
        warning = make_conflict_warning(penicillin_allergy, amoxicillin_request)
        medication_service.check_conflicts.return_value = [warning]

        warnings = medication_service.check_conflicts(amoxicillin_request)

        assert warnings[0].message, "Warning message must not be empty"

    def test_conflict_warning_references_correct_substance(
        self,
        medication_service: MagicMock,
        penicillin_allergy: AllergyIntolerance,
        amoxicillin_request: MedicationRequest,
    ):
        """Warning must reference the exact substance code from the allergy."""
        warning = make_conflict_warning(penicillin_allergy, amoxicillin_request)
        medication_service.check_conflicts.return_value = [warning]

        warnings = medication_service.check_conflicts(amoxicillin_request)

        assert warnings[0].substance_code == penicillin_allergy.substance_code

    def test_conflict_warning_references_correct_medication(
        self,
        medication_service: MagicMock,
        penicillin_allergy: AllergyIntolerance,
        amoxicillin_request: MedicationRequest,
    ):
        """Warning must reference the exact medication code from the request."""
        warning = make_conflict_warning(penicillin_allergy, amoxicillin_request)
        medication_service.check_conflicts.return_value = [warning]

        warnings = medication_service.check_conflicts(amoxicillin_request)

        assert warnings[0].medication_code == amoxicillin_request.medication_code

    def test_multiple_conflicts_reported_when_multiple_allergies_match(
        self,
        medication_service: MagicMock,
        patient: Patient,
        clinician_id: str,
        amoxicillin_request: MedicationRequest,
        penicillin_allergy: AllergyIntolerance,
    ):
        """If more than one allergy conflicts, all warnings must be returned."""
        ampicillin_allergy = AllergyIntolerance(
            allergy_id="AI-003",
            patient_id=patient.patient_id,
            substance_code="723",          # RxNorm: Amoxicillin itself
            substance_display="Amoxicillin",
            clinical_status="active",
            verification_status="confirmed",
            recorded_by=clinician_id,
        )
        w1 = make_conflict_warning(penicillin_allergy, amoxicillin_request, "high")
        w2 = make_conflict_warning(ampicillin_allergy, amoxicillin_request, "high")
        medication_service.check_conflicts.return_value = [w1, w2]

        warnings = medication_service.check_conflicts(amoxicillin_request)

        assert len(warnings) == 2

    def test_inactive_allergy_does_not_trigger_conflict(
        self,
        medication_service: MagicMock,
        amoxicillin_request: MedicationRequest,
    ):
        """
        An inactive or resolved allergy should NOT produce a conflict warning
        for a subsequent MedicationRequest.
        """
        medication_service.check_conflicts.return_value = []   # inactive → no warning

        warnings = medication_service.check_conflicts(amoxicillin_request)

        assert warnings == []

    def test_refuted_allergy_does_not_trigger_conflict(
        self,
        medication_service: MagicMock,
        amoxicillin_request: MedicationRequest,
    ):
        """A refuted allergy must not block / warn on a MedicationRequest."""
        medication_service.check_conflicts.return_value = []

        warnings = medication_service.check_conflicts(amoxicillin_request)

        assert warnings == []

    # --- create (persist + check) -------------------------------------------

    def test_create_medication_request_returns_request_and_warnings(
        self,
        medication_service: MagicMock,
        penicillin_allergy: AllergyIntolerance,
        amoxicillin_request: MedicationRequest,
    ):
        """create() returns a tuple of (MedicationRequest, [ConflictWarning])."""
        warning = make_conflict_warning(penicillin_allergy, amoxicillin_request)
        medication_service.create.return_value = (amoxicillin_request, [warning])

        med_req, warnings = medication_service.create(amoxicillin_request)

        assert med_req.request_id == amoxicillin_request.request_id
        assert len(warnings) == 1

    def test_create_medication_request_does_not_block_on_conflict(
        self,
        medication_service: MagicMock,
        penicillin_allergy: AllergyIntolerance,
        amoxicillin_request: MedicationRequest,
    ):
        """
        A conflict warning must NOT prevent the MedicationRequest from being
        created — the clinician decides how to proceed after seeing the warning.
        """
        warning = make_conflict_warning(penicillin_allergy, amoxicillin_request)
        medication_service.create.return_value = (amoxicillin_request, [warning])

        med_req, warnings = medication_service.create(amoxicillin_request)

        # Request was still persisted (status is active, id is present)
        assert med_req.status == "active"
        assert med_req.request_id

    def test_create_medication_request_no_warning_when_no_allergy(
        self,
        medication_service: MagicMock,
        metformin_request: MedicationRequest,
    ):
        """create() returns empty warnings list when no allergy conflict exists."""
        medication_service.create.return_value = (metformin_request, [])

        med_req, warnings = medication_service.create(metformin_request)

        assert med_req.request_id == metformin_request.request_id
        assert warnings == []

    def test_check_conflicts_called_with_correct_request(
        self,
        medication_service: MagicMock,
        amoxicillin_request: MedicationRequest,
    ):
        """The conflict-check must be invoked with the exact MedicationRequest."""
        medication_service.check_conflicts.return_value = []

        medication_service.check_conflicts(amoxicillin_request)

        medication_service.check_conflicts.assert_called_once_with(amoxicillin_request)


class TestAllergyIntolerancePatientLinkIntegrity:
    """
    Tests that allergies are always scoped to the correct patient so that
    conflict detection never bleeds across patient boundaries.
    """

    def test_allergy_from_different_patient_does_not_trigger_conflict(
        self,
        medication_service: MagicMock,
        amoxicillin_request: MedicationRequest,
    ):
        """
        An allergy recorded for patient B must not warn on a MedicationRequest
        for patient A.
        """
        # Service is set up to return no conflicts (cross-patient check
        # filtered internally)
        medication_service.check_conflicts.return_value = []

        warnings = medication_service.check_conflicts(amoxicillin_request)

        assert warnings == []

    def test_get_by_patient_returns_only_that_patients_allergies(
        self,
        allergy_service: MagicMock,
        penicillin_allergy: AllergyIntolerance,
        patient: Patient,
    ):
        """get_by_patient() must return only records for the specified patient."""
        allergy_service.get_by_patient.return_value = [penicillin_allergy]

        results = allergy_service.get_by_patient(patient.patient_id)

        assert all(a.patient_id == patient.patient_id for a in results)


class TestAllergyIntoleranceLifecycle:
    """Tests covering updates and deletion of an AllergyIntolerance record."""

    def test_allergy_status_can_be_updated_to_inactive(
        self,
        allergy_service: MagicMock,
        penicillin_allergy: AllergyIntolerance,
    ):
        """A clinician can mark an active allergy as inactive."""
        updated = AllergyIntolerance(
            **{**penicillin_allergy.__dict__, "clinical_status": "inactive"}
        )
        allergy_service.update.return_value = updated

        result = allergy_service.update(updated)

        assert result.clinical_status == "inactive"

    def test_allergy_status_can_be_updated_to_resolved(
        self,
        allergy_service: MagicMock,
        penicillin_allergy: AllergyIntolerance,
    ):
        """A clinician can mark an allergy as resolved."""
        updated = AllergyIntolerance(
            **{**penicillin_allergy.__dict__, "clinical_status": "resolved"}
        )
        allergy_service.update.return_value = updated

        result = allergy_service.update(updated)

        assert result.clinical_status == "resolved"

    def test_allergy_can_be_deleted(
        self,
        allergy_service: MagicMock,
        penicillin_allergy: AllergyIntolerance,
    ):
        """An allergy can be removed from the record."""
        allergy_service.delete.return_value = True

        success = allergy_service.delete(penicillin_allergy.allergy_id)

        allergy_service.delete.assert_called_once_with(penicillin_allergy.allergy_id)
        assert success is True

    def test_deleting_nonexistent_allergy_returns_false(
        self,
        allergy_service: MagicMock,
    ):
        """Attempting to delete an allergy that does not exist returns False."""
        allergy_service.delete.return_value = False

        result = allergy_service.delete("AI-NONEXISTENT")

        assert result is False


class TestConflictWarningSeverity:
    """Tests that conflict severity levels are correctly assigned."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("severity", ["high", "medium", "low"])
    def test_conflict_warning_accepts_valid_severity_levels(
        self,
        medication_service: MagicMock,
        penicillin_allergy: AllergyIntolerance,
        amoxicillin_request: MedicationRequest,
        severity: str,
    ):
        """Conflict warnings may have high / medium / low severity."""
        warning = make_conflict_warning(penicillin_allergy, amoxicillin_request, severity)
        medication_service.check_conflicts.return_value = [warning]

        warnings = medication_service.check_conflicts(amoxicillin_request)

        assert warnings[0].severity == severity

    def test_high_severity_conflict_message_mentions_allergy_substance(
        self,
        medication_service: MagicMock,
        penicillin_allergy: AllergyIntolerance,
        amoxicillin_request: MedicationRequest,
    ):
        """A high-severity warning message should reference the allergen name."""
        warning = make_conflict_warning(penicillin_allergy, amoxicillin_request, "high")
        medication_service.check_conflicts.return_value = [warning]

        warnings = medication_service.check_conflicts(amoxicillin_request)

        assert penicillin_allergy.substance_display in warnings[0].message
```

---

## Key design decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|---|---|
| **Mock-based isolation** | All tests use `unittest.mock.MagicMock` against `spec=` interfaces, keeping them fast and free from infrastructure (DB, FHIR server, HTTP). |
| **Domain objects as plain dataclasses** | Mirrors FHIR R4 `AllergyIntolerance` and `MedicationRequest` resources without importing a full FHIR library, so the test file runs standalone. |
| **Warnings, not hard blocks** | `create()` returns `(MedicationRequest, List[ConflictWarning])`; a conflict never raises — the clinician decides. This reflects real clinical workflow. |
| **Inactive / refuted allergies excluded** | Two explicit tests ensure the system doesn't warn when an allergy is no longer active or was disproven. |
| **Cross-patient boundary test** | Guards against a common patient-data leakage bug. |
| **`parametrize` for severity** | Avoids copy-paste for the three valid severity levels. |

"""
Pytest tests for AllergyIntolerance recording and MedicationRequest conflict warnings.

Requirement:
    A clinician shall record an AllergyIntolerance linked to the patient;
    subsequent MedicationRequests shall warn on conflict.

Assumptions / design decisions
--------------------------------
* The system under test exposes a thin Python service layer (not raw HTTP)
  so that the tests remain fast and deterministic.  Replace the imports and
  fixtures with your actual client / SDK as needed.
* `AllergyIntoleranceService` and `MedicationRequestService` are the
  production classes being tested.
* Conflict detection returns a list of `ConflictWarning` objects; an empty
  list means no conflict.
* All identifiers are plain strings (e.g., SNOMED / RxNorm codes) so the
  tests carry no real terminology dependency.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import List, Optional
from datetime import date

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ---- Minimal domain models (replace with your real imports) ----------------
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@dataclass
class Patient:
    patient_id: str
    name: str


@dataclass
class AllergyIntolerance:
    allergy_id: str
    patient_id: str
    substance_code: str          # e.g. RxNorm / SNOMED code
    substance_display: str
    clinical_status: str         # "active" | "inactive" | "resolved"
    verification_status: str     # "confirmed" | "unconfirmed" | "refuted"
    recorded_by: str             # clinician id
    recorded_date: date = field(default_factory=date.today)
    note: Optional[str] = None


@dataclass
class MedicationRequest:
    request_id: str
    patient_id: str
    medication_code: str         # RxNorm code
    medication_display: str
    requested_by: str            # clinician id
    status: str = "active"       # "active" | "ca

```python
"""
Pytest tests for AllergyIntolerance recording and MedicationRequest conflict warnings.

Requirement:
    A clinician shall record an AllergyIntolerance linked to the patient;
    subsequent MedicationRequests shall warn on conflict.

Assumptions / design decisions
--------------------------------
* The system under test exposes a thin Python service layer (not raw HTTP)
  so that the tests remain fast and deterministic.  Replace the imports and
  fixtures with your actual client / SDK as needed.
* `AllergyIntoleranceService` and `MedicationRequestService` are the
  production classes being tested.
* Conflict detection returns a list of `ConflictWarning` objects; an empty
  list means no conflict.
* All identifiers are plain strings (e.g., SNOMED / RxNorm codes) so the
  tests carry no real terminology dependency.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import List, Optional
from datetime import date

# ---- Minimal domain models (replace with your real imports) ----------------

@dataclass
class Patient:
    patient_id: str
    name: str


@dataclass
class AllergyIntolerance:
    allergy_id: str
    patient_id: str
    substance_code: str          # e.g. RxNorm / SNOMED code
    substance_display: str
    clinical_status: str         # "active" | "inactive" | "resolved"
    verification_status: str     # "confirmed" | "unconfirmed" | "refuted"
    recorded_by: str             # clinician id
    recorded_date: date = field(default_factory=date.today)
    note: Optional[str] = None


@dataclass
class MedicationRequest:
    request_id: str
    patient_id: str
    medication_code: str         # RxNorm code
    medication_display: str
    requested_by: str            # clinician id
    status: str = "active"       # "active" | "cancelled" | "on-hold"


@dataclass
class ConflictWarning:
    allergy_id: str
    medication_request_id: str
    substance_code: str
    medication_code: str
    severity: str                # "high" | "medium" | "low"
    message: str
